chunk_summary: This section discusses hemoglobin screening indications and iron-folic acid (IFA) pause-restart rules under the Anemia Mukt Bharat program, focusing on clinical management, therapeutic overlap, and follow-up protocols for anemia in children and adolescents.
learning_objectives: Understand the clinical indications for hemoglobin screening in children and adolescents.
Learn the therapeutic overlap rule for managing prophylactic and therapeutic iron dosing.
Identify follow-up protocols for restarting prophylactic IFA after therapeutic iron treatment.
key_facts: Hemoglobin screening is indicated for symptoms like pallor, pica, fatigue, tachycardia, and growth faltering.
Therapeutic iron dosing requires stopping prophylactic IFA until therapy is completed and side effects resolve.
Point-of-care hemoglobinometry is a screening tool; confirm borderline or severe anemia with a lab hemogram.
Prophylactic IFA should resume only after therapeutic iron treatment ends, diet stabilizes, and follow-up is planned.
Adolescents require a documented school-interface plan to ensure proper weekly IFA dosing post-therapy.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: "Measure Hb in clinic (or arrange a lab hemogram) when there is pallor, pica, fatigue, tachycardia, poor appetite, growth faltering, recurrent infections, menarche with heavy bleeding, or chronic disease that increases anemia risk." (IAP recommendations, Oct 2022)
document_key_topics: Therapeutic overlap rule for IFA and iron
Follow-up and monitoring protocols for anemia management
